title: Unit 6 Checkpoint Quiz — Passive Voice & Se Constructions

questions.prompt: What are the three types of 'se' constructions covered in this unit?
options: Passive se, impersonal se, accidental se
Reflexive se, passive se, future se
Passive se, conditional se, reflexive se
Impersonal se, subjunctive se, past se
questions.explanation: The three se constructions: (1) Passive se — actions without a stated agent (se venden tamales), (2) Impersonal se — general 'one/people' statements (se come bien), (3) Accidental se — unplanned events (se me olvidó).

questions.prompt: A sign reads 'Se busca cocinera.' What does it mean?
options: Cook wanted / We're looking for a cook.
questions.explanation: 'Se busca' = someone/something is being looked for (passive se). 'Se busca cocinera' = Cook wanted. This is a very common Mexican classified ad or sign format.

questions.prompt: In passive se, how does the verb agree?
options: With the grammatical subject (the thing being done)
questions.explanation: In passive se, the verb agrees with the subject. 'Se vende casa' (singular) vs. 'Se venden casas' (plural). This is unlike the impersonal se, which is always singular.

questions.prompt: What does 'Se me fue el camión' mean?
options: I missed the bus (it left on me — accidental).
questions.explanation: Accidental se: 'Se me fue el camión' = the bus left on me = I missed the bus. The 'me' makes it personal (it happened to ME). It implies you didn't intend to miss it.

questions.prompt: Which is the impersonal se construction?
options: En México se cena a las nueve.
questions.explanation: 'Se cena a las nueve' = people eat dinner at nine / one dines at nine — a general statement about customs. The impersonal se describes what people/one does in general, always third-person singular.

questions.prompt: When would you use the TRUE passive (ser + past participle) instead of se?
options: When you want to mention who performed the action ('by someone')
Never — it's not used in modern Spanish
questions.explanation: The true passive (ser + past participle) is used when you state who did the action: 'La carta fue escrita por Pablo' = The letter was written by Pablo. When there's no agent, se is preferred.

questions.prompt: Choose the best translation: 'I accidentally dropped my phone.'
questions.explanation: Accidental se: 'Se me cayó el celular' = my phone dropped on me (accidentally). 'Tiré el celular' implies intentional throwing. The accidental se is the perfect structure for unintended events.

questions.prompt: What does a Mexican sign reading 'Se hacen copias' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Se hacen copias' = copies are made here / we make copies. The passive se ('se hacen') expresses the service offered without mentioning who does it.
